subTopicTitle: Heart of Darkness (Conrad) — Part III — “The horror!”

items.prompt: What is the significance of Kurtz's final words, 'The horror! The horror!' in 'Heart of Darkness'?
keyPoints: Represents Kurtz's realization of the darkness within humanity
Symbolizes the culmination of his experiences and actions in the Congo
Reflects the novel's themes of existential dread and moral ambiguity

keyPoints: Marlow becomes increasingly disillusioned with European imperialism
He struggles with the moral complexities of his journey
Marlow's perception of Kurtz shifts from admiration to understanding his moral downfall
